question: I want to fine-tune ASR for my domain. Where do I start?
ground_truth: The orchestrator should run the discovery/scoping questions before recommending a technique, then outline the staged workflow and the sub-skills it will delegate to.
expected_behavior: Route to references/workflow.md and run Stage 1-2 discovery questions (real audio hours, domain text, eval set/target, latency/HW budget, deployment target).
Do not jump to a fine-tuning command before scoping and a baseline.
Explain the cheapest-sufficient-path approach and name the sub-skills for data, training, evaluation, and deployment.
Offer to answer cost/time/data questions along the way.
Announce steps (Step N/8).

question: My Riva ASR model gets our German medical jargon wrong. What's the cheapest way to fix it?
ground_truth: A known-vocabulary problem is the word-boosting rung. The orchestrator should recommend word boosting first and route configuration to the deployment sub-skill, not fine-tuning.
expected_behavior: Route to references/path-selection.md and diagnose known-jargon as the boosting rung.
Recommend word boosting (runtime, no training) before an LM or fine-tune.
Hand runtime configuration off to the nemotron-speech sub-skill.
Avoid recommending fine-tuning for a bounded known-word problem.
Advise measuring baseline vs post-change WER.

question: Boosting and an n-gram LM didn't help — the model mishears our noisy call-center audio with strong accents. What now?
ground_truth: An acoustic gap after cheaper rungs escalates to fine-tuning; the orchestrator delegates data and training to the sub-skills.
expected_behavior: Route to references/path-selection.md and recognize accents+noise as an acoustic gap.
Escalate to fine-tuning; consider data via the SDG/Data sub-skill and train via nemo-speech-asr-finetune.
Confirm sufficient transcribed audio (100+ h ideal; ~10 h only if mixed to avoid catastrophic forgetting).
Delegate evaluation (normalized WER + forgetting check) to the evaluation stage of nemo-speech-asr-finetune.
Do not restart at boosting/LM since they were already shown insufficient.

question: Which sub-skills will you use to add Farsi support to our ASR, and what does each do?
ground_truth: The orchestrator should map the stages to sub-skills (data-designer, nemo-speech-asr-finetune for training and evaluation, nemotron-speech) and flag the remaining data-profiling placeholder.
expected_behavior: Route to references/sub-skills.md.
Map data generation to data-designer (+ nemotron-speech TTS), training to nemo-speech-asr-finetune, deployment to nemotron-speech.
Use nemo-speech-asr-finetune for evaluation; flag the data-profiling skill as a placeholder with interim guidance.
Note a new language may need cross-language transfer and lots of data.
Describe the handoff inputs/outputs between orchestrator and each sub-skill.

question: How much data and how many GPU hours will it take to hit 10% WER, and should I use L40S or H100?
ground_truth: The orchestrator should answer with ranges and assumptions, refuse a fabricated hours-to-WER number, recommend a pilot, and give GPU-choice guidance.
expected_behavior: Route to references/planning-answers.md.
Explain there is no universal hours-to-WER mapping; establish baseline/target and run a small pilot to read the trend.
Give data guidance by rung (100+ h fine-tune; text-only for LM; little/none for boosting).
Give L40S vs H100 guidance based on model size, batch/precision, VRAM headroom, and cost.
State assumptions and prefer measured pilots over guesses.

question: How should the orchestrator decide whether the fine-tune worked and whether to loop or ship?
ground_truth: Evaluate normalized domain WER plus an A/B forgetting check on a general set, do error-driven analysis, then loop with targeted data or ship — consulting the user before extra cycles.
expected_behavior: Route to references/workflow.md (Stage 6-7) and use nemo-speech-asr-finetune's evaluation stage.
Report normalized WER on the domain set and a general-set forgetting delta.
Use error-driven analysis to choose the next lever and change one thing per loop.
Select/average checkpoints when the target is met; keep a blind holdout.
Consult the user before another tuning cycle.

question: I already have a fine-tuned .nemo. Convert it with nemo2riva and deploy a Riva ASR NIM with riva-build and riva-deploy.
ground_truth: Pure export/deployment of an existing model is executed by the nemotron-speech skill; the orchestrator should route there rather than driving deployment itself.
expected_behavior: Recognize this as deployment execution and defer to the nemotron-speech skill.
Do not emit nemo2riva/riva-build/riva-deploy commands from this skill.
Do not start a scoping or fine-tuning workflow for a finished model.
Optionally note this skill orchestrates the deployment stage but nemotron-speech executes it.

question: Fine-tune OpenAI Whisper with the OpenAI API on my dataset.
ground_truth: The skill should stay silent because the user explicitly requested OpenAI Whisper, not NeMo/Riva ASR customization.
expected_behavior: Do not activate nemotron-asr-finetune only because the words fine-tune and ASR appear.
Do not route to any orchestration reference.
Follow the appropriate OpenAI workflow instead.
Do not introduce NeMo/Riva sub-skills.

question: Fine-tune a Llama text LLM on my chat logs.
ground_truth: The skill should stay silent because this is text LLM fine-tuning, not ASR customization.
expected_behavior: Do not activate nemotron-asr-finetune for a text LLM fine-tuning task.
Do not route to ASR orchestration references.
Use the relevant LLM fine-tuning workflow instead.
Do not mention word boosting, WER, or ASR sub-skills.
